findings.evidence: Lines 64 and 77 expose the helper lemmas, while line 84 exposes the equivalence body; lines 93 and 101 already provide the public evaluation API, and the base-change API uses those lemmas at RootsOfUnityBaseChange.lean:84 and :97.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from `characterMulEquivRootsOfUnity` and make the two construction lemmas private again; keep public characteristic lemmas for forward/inverse evaluation.
findings.issue: `characterMulEquivRootsOfUnity` is marked `@[expose]`, and its construction helpers `mem_zpowers_generator`/`card_multiplicative_zmod` were made public even though the usable API is the generator evaluation lemmas.

findings.evidence: The forward rule has `@[simp]` at lines 92-97, while the inverse generator rule at lines 101-107 lacks it; the specialized base-change inverse rules are all tagged `@[simp]` at RootsOfUnityBaseChange.lean:89 and :101.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to this lemma so both directions of the public equivalence normalize without unfolding or manual rewrites.
findings.issue: `characterMulEquivRootsOfUnity_symm_apply_generator` is the characteristic inverse-generator rule for the now-public character equivalence, but it is not tagged `@[simp]`.

summary: The base-change points API now has the needed scalar-aware inverse lemma, but the PR still exposes implementation details and leaves one public characteristic inverse lemma out of simp normal form.
